entries.title: The Complete Sherlock Holmes Long Stories
entries.full_title: The Complete Sherlock Holmes Long Stories
entries.description:
From his first appearance in The Study in Scarlet in 1887, Sherlock Holmes has remained the most celebrated detective the world has ever known. The Holmes stories have been published in 47 languages, made into 126 films, turned into countless television series and over 4000 radio scripts.

This omnibus contains all the four long stories (each a full length novel in itself). These con- tinue to achieve an appeal and a circulation even wider than when they were first published.

What makes the Sherlock Holmes stories so immensely gripping to today's readers? Clearly everyone has his own key to Conan Doyle's genius. Graham Greene points to 'the genuine spine-creeping melodrama' in The Hound of the Baskervilles, John Fowles to 'the brilliant cover- up' in The Valley of Fear. Many would echo the view of C. P. Snow that 'the Holmes stories are a great artistic triumph of a very strange kind'.

entries.by_statement: Sir Arthur Conan Doyle ; edited with annotations by Leslie S. Klinger ; with additional research by Janet Byrne and Patricia J. Chui.

description.value: This monumental edition promises to be the most important new contribution to Sherlock Holmes literature since William Baring-Gould's 1967 classic work. In this boxed set, Leslie Klinger, a leading world authority, reassembles Arthur Conan Doyle's 56 classic short stories in the order in which they appeared in late nineteenth- and early twentieth-century book editions. Inside, readers will find a cornucopia of insights: beginners will benefit from Klinger's insightful biographies of Holmes, Watson, and Conan Doyle; history lovers will revel in the wealth of Victorian literary and cultural details; Sherlockian fanatics will puzzle over tantalizing new theories; art lovers will thrill to the 700-plus illustrations, which make this the most lavishly illustrated edition of the Holmes tales ever produced. The New Annotated Sherlock Holmes illuminates the timeless genius of Arthur Conan Doyle for an entirely new generation of readers. 700+ illustrations.

first_sentence.value: IN THE YEAR 1878 I took my degree of Doctor of Medicine of the University of London, and proceeded to Netley to go through the course prescribed for surgeons in the Army.

description.value: This book contains the four novels about Sherlock Holmes, and with its companion volume, 'The Complete Sherlock Holmes Short Stories', comprises all the cases of the famous detective.
